title: Eriosyce engleri
taxonomy: Eriosyce engleri is classified as a species of cactus, specifically within the Eriosyce genus. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and the family Cactaceae. This plant is native to the Atacama Desert in Chile and is a type of columnar cactus. The Eriosyce genus is part of the broader classification of Caryophyllales, an order of flowering plants. Eriosyce engleri is also known as a type of cold-hardy cactus due to its ability to tolerate frost.

intro: Eriosyce engleri, also known as the 'Engler's Pine Cactus,' is a small to medium-sized, globular cactus species native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Eriosyce engleri include flavonoids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions and decoctions, often used to treat digestive issues and provide antioxidant benefits.
